idea-plugin.name: SQL Dialect for Apache Doris
idea-plugin.description:
First-class Apache Doris support for DataGrip and IntelliJ IDEA Ultimate — a dedicated SQL dialect, a multi-catalog database tree, table-function completion, and a Stop button that actually cancels your Doris query. Compatible with the 2026.1 and 2026.2 IDE lines.

  • Multi-catalog database tree. Doris external catalogs (Hive, Iceberg, JDBC, ...) appear as a real catalog → database → table hierarchy, with three-part catalog.db.table completion and resolution and a stepped console catalog/database switcher
  • Table-valued-function completion. tasks(), jobs(), mv_infos(), partitions(), backends() and more complete their property keys and enum values, and their output columns resolve — including schema-by-argument (tasks("type"="mv"))
  • Reliable query cancel. The Stop button kills the running Doris query even behind a load balancer, where the driver's own cancel silently misses
  • Dedicated DorisSQL dialect with correct statement and run-block boundaries for Doris syntax the generic MySQL dialect mis-parses: INSERT OVERWRITE, SELECT * EXCEPT(...), QUALIFY, CREATE JOB, materialized views, DISTRIBUTED BY / PROPERTIES DDL, REFRESH, WARM UP, and more — plus native typed parsing for navigation inside DDL bodies
  • Window functions, CTEs, Doris cast targets (STRING, LARGEINT, ARRAY<...>, MAP<...>), TRY_CAST, and map access (col['key']) parse with full structure and completion
  • Completion for 825 Doris built-in functions and Doris data types (VARIANT, BITMAP, HLL, AGG_STATE, ...)
  • Doris-accurate syntax validation via an embedded build of the Doris SQL parser
  • Ready-to-use Apache Doris data-source template: MySQL Connector/J download, default port 9030, connection validation, SHOW CREATE definitions

This is an independent, community plugin developed by Sortdev SRL. It is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by the Apache Software Foundation. "Apache Doris", Doris, and the Doris logo are trademarks of the Apache Software Foundation, used here only to identify the database this plugin supports. For the official project see doris.apache.org.

Source, issues and documentation: github.com/sort-dev/doris-intellij-plugin
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.6.0 — enhanced query cancelling across console windows.
  • Cancel works across console windows: with several consoles running queries against the same data source, Stop now kills exactly the query of the console you pressed it in — the other consoles' queries keep running. (Previously the plugin refused to guess in that situation and fell back to the IDE's stock cancel, which could show the "Deactivate the Data Source?" dialog.) Repeat cancels are also faster.
  • Function completion shows each function's kind: scalar, aggregate, window, and table functions get distinct icons and labels, sourced from Doris's own function registry (via the shared brikk-sql-metadata catalog).
0.5.0 — reliable query cancel, native typed parsing, calmer completion.
  • Reliable query cancel (on by default): the Stop button now actually kills the running Doris query — even behind a load balancer, where the driver's own cancel can miss the frontend or kill the wrong session. The console session stays alive, and the spurious "Cancelling Failed. Deactivate the Data Source?" dialog no longer appears when the cancel succeeds. Opt out with -Ddoris.cancel.experimental=false.
  • Native typed parsing (on by default): Doris DDL/statements parse into real typed structure for navigation inside CREATE VIEW/TABLE/ MATERIALIZED VIEW/JOB bodies, falling back safely to the lenient parse on anything it can't cleanly type. Opt out with -Ddoris.replay.poc=false.
  • Function completion no longer interrupts typing: function suggestions auto-pop only in expression positions, so keywords and literals (e.g. GROUP BY 1, ... AS name) type cleanly. Explicit completion (Ctrl+Space) still offers the full list everywhere.
  • More Doris DDL variants recognized (aggregate/unique-key tables, dynamic/list/range partitioning, routine load, catalog/resource/workload DDL, UPDATE/DELETE), and more * EXCEPT(...) false-error cases silenced.
0.4.0 — 2026.2-ready, plus introspection and editor fixes.
  • Compatible with the upcoming 2026.2 IDE line (one artifact spans 2026.1 and 2026.2; compatibility range raised accordingly).
  • Fixed an IDE-internal error ("Session not started") that could appear during multi-catalog introspection.
  • SELECT * EXCEPT(...): two more false-error cases silenced — UNION operand-count mismatches and "ambiguous column" when the column is in the EXCEPT list. Genuine errors still flagged.
  • Completion after a table alias (v.) now offers only that relation's columns — no more stray function suggestions.
0.3.0 — catalogs, table-valued functions, and a much quieter editor.
  • Multi-catalog support (on by default): Doris external catalogs (Hive, Iceberg, JDBC, ...) appear as a real catalog → database → table tree. The internal catalog introspects automatically; enable external catalogs per-catalog in the schemas pane. Three-part catalog.db.table references resolve and complete, the console switcher picks catalog then database, and real Doris column types (variant, array<...>, ...) display throughout. Opt out with the VM option -Ddoris.catalogs.experimental=false.
  • Upgrade note: existing Doris data sources rebuild their cached model once (silently) on first launch — the tree repopulates on next connect/refresh.
  • Table-valued function completion: tasks(), jobs(), mv_infos(), partitions(), backends() and friends complete their property keys and enum values, and their output columns resolve — including schema-by-argument (tasks("type"="mv")). File TVFs (s3(), hdfs(), ...) stay quiet without fabricating columns.
  • SELECT * EXCEPT(...) no longer raises false errors (column-count mismatch and dependent noise suppressed; genuine mismatches still flagged).
  • Quieter editor across real Doris SQL: session variables, USE @group, SWITCH, materialized-view schedules, CREATE JOB, partition specs, GROUP BY aliases, CTAS key clauses.
  • View columns now introspect correctly (completion inside queries over views).
  • Experimental (off by default): native typed parsing for Doris DDL/statements via -Ddoris.replay.poc=true.
0.2.3 — first public release.
  • DorisSQL dialect on the MySQL foundation with correct statement/run-block boundaries for Doris-specific syntax
  • Native parsing for window functions, IF(), CTE chains, and INSERT OVERWRITE pipelines
  • SELECT * EXCEPT(...), Doris cast targets, TRY_CAST, and REGEXP(...) handled without breaking resolution or completion
  • 825-function completion, Doris data types, Doris-accurate error checking
  • Apache Doris driver template with automatic MySQL Connector/J download

action.description: Execute the selection exactly as one statement
Show the canonical Doris SQL this pipe program transpiles to, without running it
Transpile and execute only the pipe stages up to and including the one under the caret
